meta.title: Tajweed Basics
meta.subtitle: Rules of proper Quranic recitation — start with these essentials.

items.rule: Each Arabic letter has a fixed exit point in the mouth or throat.
items.example: ق from deepest part of tongue; ك from palate.
items.tip: Mixing qāf and kāf is a common beginner error.

items.rule: ح and ه are different — ح is breathy from middle throat.
items.tip: Place hand on throat; feel vibration on ح.

items.rule: Iẓhār — clear n sound before throat letters (ء ه ع ح غ خ).
Idghām — merge n into following letter (ي ر م ل و ن).
Iqlāb — turn n into m before bā.
Ikhfa — hide n with ghunnah before 15 letters.
Natural madd (2 counts): vowel + alif/wāw/yā of lengthening.
Madd munfaṣil: madd letter at end of word + hamza at start of next.
Bounce ق ط ب ج د when they have sukūn.
items.tip: Not a full second vowel — a echo.

items.rule: Heavy (tafkhīm) lām after fatḥa or ḍamma: اللَّهُ
Light lām after kasra: بِسْمِ اللَّه
